sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 10" width and 7.25" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Single lamination pass in either orientation. No laminator complications.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 10" × 7.25". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

- **Orientation A** (7.25" across feed, 10" in feed direction): 7.25" across the 28" print bed → up to 3 labels across (3 × 7.25" = 21.75" ≤ 28"). At qty 2: both labels nest side-by-side in one print run.
- **Orientation B** (10" across feed, 7.25" in feed direction): 10" across the 28" bed → up to 2 labels across (2 × 10" = 20" ≤ 28"). Also fits for qty 2.

At qty 2 (current order): both labels produced in a single print run, single lam pass, single cut run.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label — Safety Yellow Flood Coat Assumption

> Material cost version date: 2026-05-28. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (verified 2026-04-22).

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.503 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.61 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.503 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.12 |
| Eco-solvent ink (Safety Yellow flood coat) ¹ | High/flood coverage — 2.5× standard rate | $0.60 |
| **Total per label** | | **$1.33** |

¹ **Flood coat ink estimate:** Standard medium-coverage ink rate at 11.13" × 7.88" (0.609 sq ft) is ~$0.27/label = ~$0.44/sq ft. At 0.503 sq ft with standard coverage: $0.22. A Safety Yellow flood coat (full background coverage across the label) applies approximately 2.5× the standard ink load. Estimate: $0.22 × 2.5 = $0.55, rounded up to $0.60 for conservatism. This is the conservative (high-cost) assumption for pricing purposes. Laminate rate updated 2026-05-28 to $0.2389/sq ft (Flexcon FLX000233 invoice verified).

**If Sean selects black-on-white (artwork version — no flood coat):** Ink drops to ~$0.25 (standard medium coverage). Material cost reduces to ~$0.98/label. Margin improves ~5 points at qty 20 (~83% → ~88%). Pricing holds unchanged — no reprice required.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **4-ROUND AI VALIDATION: COMPLETE.** 6 models × 4 rounds = 24 total model runs. Validation modified the tier table and current-order price. See Step 4 below for full documentation.

---

## Step 1 — Spec Summary and Band Classification

- Dimensions: 10" × 7.25" = 0.503 sq ft. Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. 1 production pass.
- At the low end of the singles band scope (~0.5–2.0 sq ft). Above the tiny-label threshold (<0.1 sq ft). The singles per-sq-ft band applies.
- Safety Yellow flood coat assumed (see color conflict below). Material cost: $1.70/label.

---

## Step 2 — Singles Band Rate

The singles Pricing Profile has 1 confirmed data point:

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | Rate |
|------|-------|----------------|------|
| 1230820 (Load Chart, D105) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43/sq ft |

**Band width:** Wide (1 data point). Future items in the 0.5–2.0 sq ft range should land within ±15% of $15.43/sq ft ($13.12–$17.74).

Scaling 1082570 at 0.503 sq ft:

`0.503 sq ft × $15.43/sq ft = $7.76` → rounded to **$8.00 at qty 20**

Per-sq-ft check: $8.00 / 0.503 = **$15.91/sq ft** — within ±15% of band center ✓

---

## Step 3 — Full Tier Table Construction

Tier structure proportioned from P/N 1230820's accepted tier ratios relative to the qty 20 anchor:

| Tier | 1230820 Ratio | 1082570 Raw | Quoted |
|------|--------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $30/$20 = 1.50× | $8 × 1.50 = $12.00 | **$12** |
| 10-19 | $24/$20 = 1.20× | $8 × 1.20 = $9.60 | **$10** |
| 20-49 | 1.00× (anchor) | $8.00 | **$8** |
| 50-99 | $17/$20 = 0.85× | $8 × 0.85 = $6.80 | **$7** |
| 100-199 | $14/$20 = 0.70× | $8 × 0.70 = $5.60 | **$6** |
| 200+ | $11/$20 = 0.55× | $8 × 0.55 = $4.40 | **$5** (rounded up — see note) |

**200+ tier note (pre-validation):** $4.40 proportionally rounds to $4. However, at material cost $1.70, a $4 tier yields ~58% margin — below the category minimum-worthwhile floor (~64%). Preliminary: rounded up to $5. See Step 4 — AI validation replaced this tier table with final validated figures.

**This is the pre-validation preliminary tier table.** AI validation (Step 4) modified all tiers — particularly 1-9 (raised from $12 to $16.50), 50-99 (reduced from $7 to $6.25), 100-199 (reduced from $6 to $5.25), and 200+ (reduced from $5 to $4.25 as volume reward for strategic account). The qty 20-49 anchor ($8.00) was confirmed unchanged.

---

## Step 4 — Current Order Pricing: Option A vs Option B + AI Validation (4 Rounds, 6 Models)

### The Core Question

At qty 2, what price does this order receive?

**Option A:** Apply the $55 account minimum-worthwhile-charge floor ($55.00 total, $27.50/label implied).

**Option B:** Price on job economics with setup recovery — a flat rate anchored to what the job warrants at this label size and order quantity.

**Option A argument:** Consistent with prior one-off small-run orders on this account (P/N 1277970-1278000 program, P/N 3017583, P/N 3017584).

**Option B argument:** Those precedents are tiny-label one-offs (0.008–0.026 sq ft) where the per-sq-ft band is inapplicable. At 0.503 sq ft, 1082570 is a full singles-band label — Sean can make a direct D105 comparison. A $55 total ($27.50/label implied) = $54.67/sq ft, vs D105 at $15.43/sq ft. That is 3.5× the benchmark rate and is indefensible if Sean runs the math.

---

### Round 1 — Build (6 Models)

- Qty 2 consensus: **$38–$45 cluster**
- Qty 20 consensus: **$8.50–$11.00 cluster**
- **All 6 rejected the $55 floor** for a 0.503 sq ft label vs D105 comparison
- All 6 agreed setup-recovery logic applies to the current order, not sq ft band alone
- All 6 confirmed the 20-49 anchor at approximately $8.00–$9.00/label

---

### Round 2 — Destruction (6 Models)

- **All 6 found the $55 floor indefensible** at this label size — D105 comparison available and damaging
- All 6 identified the 20-49 tier as most competitively exposed vs D105
- **All 6 recommended Option B** (job economics) over Option A ($55 floor)
- Consensus qty 2 total: **$40–$42**
- Consensus qty 20 tier: **$7.25–$8.50/label**
- Consensus 1-9 tier: **$14–$18/label**
  - One model specifically recommended $16.50 to widen the gap between the flat current-order price and the low-end catalog tier — noted for Round 4

---

### Round 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 Models)

- **5 of 6 models approved $42 for qty 2 without question** — instant approval below $50–$100 threshold
- D105 comparison at qty 20: $15.91/sq ft vs $15.43/sq ft = **3% delta** — every model called it a non-issue
- Job economics explanation (setup recovery on a 2-label run) was the **single most cited approval driver** across all 6 models
- Mental model Sean builds: ~$15.50/sq ft at qty 20, $40–$55 short-run floor — healthy framework for future quotes
- 1 outlier recommended $24–$26 total — **rejected**; ignores setup recovery and undercuts the account's own value structure

---

### Round 4 — Final Synthesis (6 Models)

- **5 of 6 unanimous: send as shown, no changes**
- 1 model recommended bumping the 1-9 tier from $16.00 to **$16.50** to widen the gap at low quantities — **adopted**
- No tier flagged as wrong by majority
- Long-term precedent: holds cleanly for 20+ items on this account without creating pricing friction
- No discomfort flags on any tier or on the $42 flat current-order price
- **No MOQ.** One model raised the possibility of a minimum order quantity. Nick's decision: **no MOQ on this account**. Elliott is a strategic account with major growth potential — access at any quantity is intentional and permanent.

---

### Final Validated Tier Table

| Tier | Price/Label | Per Sq Ft | Margin | Notes |
|------|------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| 1-9 | $16.50 | $32.80 | ~92% | Widened from preliminary $12 |
| 10-19 | $10.50 | $20.88 | ~87% | |
| 20-49 | $8.00 | $15.91 | ~83% | Band anchor confirmed — D105 delta +3%, non-issue |
| 50-99 | $6.25 | $12.43 | ~79% | |
| 100-199 | $5.25 | $10.44 | ~75% | |
| 200+ | $4.25 | $8.45 | ~69% | Intentionally steepest volume discount — reward for strategic account |

**200+ tier:** $8.45/sq ft is intentionally below the D105 anchor ($15.43/sq ft). Volume reward for a strategic account — Nick's decision. The ~69% margin floor reflects the steepest tier discount by design. Not a margin error; documented as a strategic override. (Margins updated 2026-05-28 — laminate rate corrected to $0.2389/sq ft; new material cost $1.33 vs prior $1.70.)

**No MOQ:** No minimum order quantity has been set, and none should ever be set on this account, this item, or any future Elliott item. This is a permanent account-level rule — do not add, suggest, or imply MOQ language anywhere.

**The $42.00 current-order price does NOT enter the singles Pricing Profile band.** The band tracks production-volume acceptance. One-off tied-ship orders are excluded from band calculations.

---

## Step 5 — Safety Yellow Flood Coat Assumption

Pricing assumes Safety Yellow flood coat (drawing note 6 version — higher cost, conservative). If Sean selects black-on-white (artwork version), the pricing holds unchanged and margin improves ~4 points at qty 20. The conservative assumption ensures that regardless of Sean's color selection, the pricing covers cost.

Details in Nesting and Material Cost section.

>
> **Artwork as provided:** Black linework and text on a white background — NO Safety Yellow flood coat present in the artwork.
>
> **These two are in direct conflict.** No assumption can be made that one source is authoritative without Sean's explicit confirmation.
>
> **Current status — samples delivered:**
> - Nick sent Sean physical samples of both versions (black on white per artwork; Safety Yellow per drawing note 6).
> - Samples were delivered within **~26 hours** of the request.
> - Sean has **not yet confirmed** which version he wants.
> - PO has **not yet been received.**
> - Production is **blocked** pending Sean's color selection via PO.
>
> **Until Sean selects a version:**
> - Pricing assumes Safety Yellow flood coat (conservative — higher ink coverage, higher cost).
> - Material cost used: $1.33/label (flood coat). If black-on-white is selected, actual material is ~$0.98/label.
> - If Sean selects Safety Yellow: pricing holds exactly as quoted.
> - If Sean selects black-on-white: pricing holds; margin improves ~4 points at qty 20 (~79% → ~83%). No reprice required.
> - **Under NO circumstances produce this label without Sean's explicit color version selection.**
>
> **Pricing is conservative.** The more expensive version was assumed. Pricing covers cost under either outcome.

---

> ## ⚠ CURRENT ORDER — $42.00 FLAT — JOB ECONOMICS FRAMING
>
> Sean Finn ordered qty 2 for SO 20125600 — a specific I70 unit shipping. PO not yet received (internal reference only).
>
> **This order is quoted at $42.00 flat.** Framing: job economics — setup recovery on a 2-label run. NOT a per-label rate. NOT a floor-anchored price. NOT a band data point. NOT a benchmark for future orders.
>
> **Quote framing:** "The job economics of a 2-label run include a setup-recovery component — $42.00 total for this order." Do NOT say "$21/label." Do NOT reference sq ft, the D105 comparison, or any floor price in the quote.
>
> **No minimum order quantity** on this account. Do not add, suggest, or imply MOQ language.
>
> The $42.00 price does NOT enter the singles Pricing Profile band. At reorder (any quantity): apply the tier table directly.

---

> ## DIMENSION SOURCE — ENGINEERING MANAGER CONFIRMATION
>
> No dimensions appear on the drawing (dated 08-25-04). Width 10" and Height 7.25" were confirmed by Derrik Walton, P.E., Engineering Manager at Elliott Equipment Company, via email on 2026-05-28. Walton referenced the I50 chart as the size standard for the I70 model.
>
> These dimensions are engineering-manager confirmed, not estimated. If a future drawing revision surfaces different dimensions, contact Nick before any reprice or reorder.

---

> ## MYLAR TERMINOLOGY
>
> Drawing calls out "1 mil clear Mylar overlay." Mylar is a DuPont-originated trade name for biaxially oriented polyester (BoPET) film. It maps directly to the standard 1-mil clear polyester overlaminate used across the entire Orajet 3951 material family on this account. No material substitution. No ambiguity.
>
> Consistent with the same terminology note documented on P/N 3017584 (same "Mylar overlay" wording). Nick is actively working with Elliott to standardize spec-sheet verbiage toward "1-mil polyester overlaminate."

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

---

> ## AI VALIDATION — COMPLETE
>
> 4-round, 6-model AI validation (24 total model runs per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`) was completed following the initial quote session. Full documentation in Pricing Derivation, Step 4.
>
> **Key outcomes:** $55 floor rejected unanimously → $42.00 flat adopted for qty 2 (job economics). Tier table modified from preliminary: $16.50 / $10.50 / $8.00 / $6.25 / $5.25 / $4.25. No MOQ — Nick's decision, permanent account rule.

---

>
> Sean's response to the physical sample delivery went beyond color selection. He asked Nick to suggest what specs to call out on a drawing for label/decal procurement.
>
> More significantly: **Sean and his manager are initiating a project to develop an engineering and procurement standard for labels and decals at Elliott Equipment Company.** They will be reaching out to Nick for input on the standard.

> - Nick's involvement at the engineering/specification level positions Pro Label as the expert vendor of record, not a commodity supplier.
> - This initiative is separate from the current color conflict resolution. It is a long-term relationship signal.
> - Any future Claude Code session working this account should be aware of this when framing quotes, communications, or strategic recommendations.

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Cardinal Red stock. 2 labels across, cut in rows.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around "E160" block letters. 4 large characters, no fine interior detail, open geometry.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)

## Step 1 — Process Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 total independent responses. Atomic session model: each prompt is self-contained; no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Claude Chat synthesizes after each wave and generates the next prompt. Nick is sole decision-maker; the 24 responses are evidence, not votes.

**Item:** 1210810 LBL - DANGER FALLING JIB. 10.5" × 4" = 0.292 sq ft. Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. Safety Red flood coat ANSI Z535.1/Z535.4 DANGER label. 1 production pass.

The validated $/sq ft is INTENTIONALLY ABOVE the singles band — the structurally required small-format premium per Wave 4 verdict.

## Step 2 — Wave 1: Build Round

**6 models, atomic prompts. 1 model disqualified for calculation error → 5 valid responses.**

- 4 of 5 valid responses landed **$4.51–$4.64 at qty 20**, clustering in the singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft on a per-sq-ft basis).
- Central tendency: **~$4.58 at qty 20**.
- All models respected the band floor — none priced below $15.43/sq ft.
- 1 outlier at **$6.00 at qty 20**, arguing small-format premium ABOVE band on first-principles grounds.

**Wave 1 finding:** Independent build anchors clustered at band floor. The outlier argued the small-format premium explicitly. Claude Chat carried both signals into Wave 2.

## Step 3 — Wave 2: Destruction Round

**All 6 responses returned.**

| Attack Vector | All 6 Models |
|---------------|--------------|
| Buyer attack severity | **H** (6/6) |
| Competitor attack severity | **H** (6/6) |
| Strategic attack severity | **H** (6/6) |

**Primary finding (universal):** The 10-19 tier (as configured in the proposed table going into Wave 2) was priced BELOW benchmark $/sq ft at the same tier. A smaller label carrying lower $/sq ft than a larger label inverts the cost logic and is detectable by a data-literate buyer normalizing spend across the account. Structural inversion.

**Secondary finding (universal):** $0.55 material cost (as it stood going into Wave 2) understated the true cost for a Safety Red flood coat ANSI label. Ink at the high-coverage rate needs to be in the material build, not a placeholder.

**Verdict:** 5 of 6 — Yes with modifications. 1 of 6 — No.

**Wave 2 carry-forward:** The 10-19 inversion is non-negotiable — must be corrected before quote. Material cost must be revised to include realistic high-coverage ink.

## Step 4 — Wave 3: Buyer Simulation

**All 6 responses returned. All 6 models simulated Sean Finn (Elliott Equipment Employee-Owner, ~$140K/year program, $/sq ft mental model, deepening partnership phase).**

- **All 6 calculated $15.41/sq ft at qty 20** independently and recognized benchmark consistency vs the singles band.

- **All 6 confirmed:** They are logging $15.41/sq ft as a **permanent account data point** for benchmarking every future Pro Label quote against.

**Wave 3 carry-forward:** $4.50 clears Sean's approval. The Wave 2 structural inversion (10-19 below benchmark $/sq ft) still has not been addressed in the proposed tier table. Wave 4 must do the binary check.

## Step 5 — Wave 4: Final Synthesis

**All 6 responses returned. Verdict: NO on the Wave 3 table — UNANIMOUS.**

**Primary finding (universal):** The Wave 3 table had **10-19 at $4.95 ($16.95/sq ft)**, which is **8.5% BELOW** the benchmark's same-tier $/sq ft ($18.52/sq ft for 1230820's 10-19 tier at $24.00/1.296 sq ft).

**Fix range across the 6 models:** **$5.25–$5.95**. Center: **$5.40**.

**Wave 4 verdict — unanimous NO.** The 10-19 inversion must be corrected. Every model insisted.

Nick reviewed the Wave 4 unanimous verdict and the 24-response evidence base. His decision:

**Raise the full table above benchmark $/sq ft at every tier** to ensure the small-format premium is correct and unambiguous. This is stronger than the Wave 4 minimum fix — it is the structurally clean answer that propagates the same principle to every tier, not just 10-19.

## Step 8 — Invoice Protection at the 10-19 / 20-49 Boundary

The raised 10-19 tier creates an arithmetic cliff at the 19/20 boundary:

A buyer ordering 19 units is invoiced at $95.00 (the 20 × $4.75 floor of the next tier), not $109.25. The cliff is invisible to the buyer. Required quote language carries this assurance: "You will never be invoiced more for a smaller quantity."

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **⚠ ONE-OFF PROGRAM. NOT A CATALOG PRICE. NOT A BENCHMARK.** Sean Finn verbatim: *"this will be a one off for a custom build we are doing."* The $2.75/label number is a job-economics floor for a 20-label minimum-run program — it must NEVER be used as a comparable, anchor, or benchmark for any future Elliott item, including future one-off programs. Future one-offs price from their own job economics (material + production time), not from this number. The implied ~$343/sq ft is a minimum-run artifact, not a per-sq-ft rate.

**Methodology:** Job economics for a one-off minimum-run program. Built from material cost + production time, anchored to the account's minimum-worthwhile-charge floor.

**Job inputs:**

| Component | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Material cost (full 20-label job) | $4.85 (3.125 sq ft Orajet 3951 + 2.8125 sq ft laminate + ~$0.40 ink — laminate at $0.2389/sq ft, updated 2026-05-28) |
| File prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press | **$0** — Elliott supplies production-ready DWG (account-level rule, see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Production time (file import + print/lam/cut/inspect/package) | ~25 min total (5 min file import + 20 min run) |
| Lamination passes | 1 |
| Cut runs | 1 |

**Why $55 program total:** $55 is the lowest first article price on the account (P/N 1230820 FA at $55) and is treated here as a program-level minimum-worthwhile-charge floor — the lowest number Pro Label will run a custom one-off for. It is the floor, NOT a per-label comparable. Anchoring at this floor:

- Program revenue: $55
- Material cost: ~$6.94
- Production time: ~25 min
- Contribution margin: ~$48 to cover the ~25 minutes of machine + operator time + overhead
- That is the worthwhile-charge threshold for a 4-design one-off at this scale; below it the job is not worth running

**Why the singles sq ft band is inapplicable:** At 0.008 sq ft, the singles band ($15.43/sq ft at qty 20) yields $0.12/label — nonsensical, does not cover the run.

**Why this is NOT a benchmark:**

- It is a one-off, not a catalog item. Sean stated non-recurring intent.
- The per-sq-ft implied rate (~$343/sq ft) is structurally meaningless — it is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing a minimum-run floor by a tiny label area.
- Using this item to anchor a future tiny-label quote would either (a) under-price if the future job has a different design count / footprint / production routing, or (b) over-price if the future job nests inside an existing run.
- Future tiny-label one-offs must rebuild job economics from scratch (material + production time on their actual footprint), then anchor to whichever account floor is appropriate at the time. Do NOT shortcut through this item.

**Tier structure:** All six tiers flattened to $2.75. There is no volume tier for a one-off — Sean ordered qty 5 and will not reorder. The flat tier table is a structural placeholder; the operational price is the program total.

**Profile Validation:** Outside the Printed + Laminated singles Pricing Profile band by design. Founding data point for the sub-0.1 sq ft size class is preserved on this item ONLY in the sense that this item documents the one-off pricing approach — the **price itself is NOT a Profile data point** and must NOT be fed into the singles or any other band.

**AI Validation:** Full 4-round, 6-model validation completed (24 total model runs) per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`. Verdict: unanimous **send $55 program total as shown**.

| Round | Purpose | Result |
|-------|---------|--------|
| 1 — Build | Establish baseline from scratch | **Unanimous $55** across all 6 models. Each independently anchored to the account FA floor (P/N 1230820 FA at $55), recognized the singles sq ft band as inapplicable at 0.008 sq ft, and converged on minimum-program-charge logic. |
| 2 — Destruction | Attack from hostile angles | **5 of 6 Yes / Yes with modifications.** One outlier model (1 of 6) recommended $38 program total on a pure cost-plus basis. Outlier rejected — see "Rejected $38 alternative" below. Surviving modifications were quote-language refinements only, not price changes. |
| 3 — Buyer Simulation | Role-play Sean's reaction | **All 6 models approved without questions.** All 6 placed the instant-approval threshold at **$75 or under** for a 20-label one-off program. The $55 quote lands comfortably inside that threshold. No buyer pushback predicted. No per-label math expected to be triggered if the quote is presented as a single program line. |
| 4 — Final Synthesis | Validate the final decision | **All 6 unanimous: Yes, send as shown.** No tier-level concerns. No long-term precedent concerns (item is explicitly excluded from being a benchmark by category file and item-level warnings). No discomfort flags. |

**Rejected $38 alternative.** One Round-2 model proposed $38 program total on cost-plus reasoning: material ($6.94) + ~25 min production × ~$60/hr loaded labor (~$25) + overhead (~$6) ≈ $38. Rejected because:

- Cost-plus logic ignores the existing account floor. Pro Label has already established $55 as the lowest worthwhile charge on this account (P/N 1230820 FA). Quoting a one-off custom program below the catalog FA floor would undercut the account's own price structure.
- Cost-plus logic ignores relationship context. The customer is actively displacing the incumbent and has confirmed non-recurring intent — there is no reorder runway to amortize a thin one-off margin against.
- The $17 spread between $38 and $55 is below any buyer's sensitivity threshold for a 20-label program (Round 3 confirmed instant-approval up to $75). Capturing the extra $17 has zero customer-relationship cost.
- $55 protects the floor for the next one-off; $38 sets a precedent that future tiny one-offs must beat to stay aligned.

**Validated quote language (send verbatim — Round 4 confirmed no changes needed):**

> Outrigger switch label program — 4 P/Ns (Horizontal Front, Vertical Front, Horizontal Rear, Vertical Rear), qty 5 each, 20 labels total. Cast 4-mil vinyl, black on white, 1-mil clear polyester overlay, kiss cut. Customer-supplied production-ready DWG files — no artwork or setup fees applied.
>
> One-time minimum program charge for custom build support: **$55.00 total**.
>
> This is a one-time program price for your custom build and is not a catalog or reorder rate. If any of these become repeat-production items, we'll requote at production quantities — expect pricing in the $0.35–$0.55/label range at qty 250+.

**Override:** None.

**Repricing history:**
1. Initial quote 2026-05-27 AM: $7/label = $140 program total. Based on a job-floor estimate.
2. Reprice 2026-05-27 PM: $15/label = $300 program total. Used "file prep × 4 unique designs" as a major cost driver (~$60-80 of labor).
3. **Correction 2026-05-27 evening (this version): $2.75/label = $55 program total.** Elliott supplies production-ready DWG; file prep is NOT a cost input on this account. The $15/label reprice was built on an incorrect assumption that has now been purged account-wide. With file prep at $0, true job inputs are ~$6.94 material + ~25 min production time. The worthwhile-charge floor is the account's lowest FA price ($55), not a reconstructed labor-heavy figure.

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 15" Cardinal Red stock. 1 label across (14.375" height across 15" roll, 0.625" clearance). Single continuous cut path of the "ELLIOTT" wordmark.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 7 block characters. Large simple letters with open counters (E ×3, O ×1) — straightforward weeding, no fine interior detail.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 30" tape allows 2 labels side-by-side per masking pass (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" across 30" tape, 1.25" waste strip).
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 70.8125" length.
6. **Package** — flat or rolled (depending on logistics), masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application on crane body/boom.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (15" Cardinal Red stock; 28" max cut width — well within tolerance for the 70.8125" feed length)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low–medium. 7 large block characters at large scale, open counters. Simpler geometry per sq ft than the "E190" / "E160" / "D115" model designations (which are similar-scale characters at smaller overall size). The increased physical scale slows weeding linearly with area, but the open-counter geometry keeps the weeding rate per inch comparable.
- **Cut feed length:** ~76.8" per label (label + 6" spacing). On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), ~23 labels per roll (1800 ÷ 76.8 = 23.4 → 23 complete labels with end waste).

sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Sq ft scaling from the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster benchmark (P/N 1205720) with a 19.3% $/sq ft step-down to reflect the structural advantages of a large-format size class. Validated through the full 4-wave AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` (24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves).

**Sq ft comparison:**

| Item | Dimensions | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft |
|------|-----------|-------|----------------|---------|
| P/N 1205720 (small-format benchmark) | 33-9/16" × 11" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3017435 (small-format parity) | 43.91" × 8.38" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3018378 (small-format parity) | 32.88" × 11.00" | 2.512 | $35 | $13.94 |
| P/N 1186310 (small-format parity) | 33-9/16" × 11" | 2.564 | $35 | $13.65 |
| **P/N 3010704 (large-format founding data point)** | **70.8125" × 14.375"** | **7.069** | **$78** | **$11.03** |

**Per sq ft at qty 20:** $78 ÷ 7.069 = **$11.03/sq ft**

**Step-down from small-format band:** $13.67/sq ft → $11.03/sq ft = **19.3% step-down**. Justified by three structural advantages of the large-format size class:
1. **Fixed labor amortization** — file import, machine setup, inspection, packaging are roughly constant per label regardless of physical size. The fixed-labor share of cost per sq ft drops as label area grows.
2. **Simpler weeding geometry per sq ft** — large block letters with open counters weed faster per sq ft than dense small-format text. The 7-character "ELLIOTT" wordmark with E ×3 and O ×1 open counters is geometrically simpler than a comparable area of small-format model designations.
3. **Lower material cost per sq ft** — $2.83/sq ft on this item vs $3.41/sq ft on the 2.5 sq ft benchmark. The 30" application tape amortizes 2-up against the 15" single-label vinyl feed, reducing tape cost per sq ft by ~$0.08/sq ft.

**Profile Validation:** Establishes the **Large-Format Cut Vinyl band (5+ sq ft)** in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at **$11.03/sq ft at qty 20**. This is a separate band from the existing 2.51–2.56 sq ft concession-phase band ($13.65–$13.94/sq ft). The two bands are independent — they do not contaminate or interact with each other. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster only, not to this large-format class.

## 4-Wave Validation Record (3010704)

> **NOTE:** Validation was conducted at 13.5" height (6.639 sq ft). Nick corrected the operative height to 14.375" (7.069 sq ft) after validation. Material cost is feed-length-driven, not area-driven — the vinyl cost ($18.51) depends on the 76.8125" feed length, which is unchanged; the tape cost ($1.51) depends on the same feed length and still nests 2-up on the 30" tape (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" ≤ 30"). All prices and margins are unchanged. $/sq ft figures were recalculated with the larger area (denominator change only).

**Wave 1 — Build (6 models, independent, atomic prompts):**
- Price range at qty 20: $63–$91. Central cluster: $75–$91. Median: ~$83.
- Fundamental debate: $/sq ft step-down at 2.6× the calibrated size.
- 2 models held band at $13.71/sq ft ($91). 4 models applied step-downs ($63–$85).
- All 6 confirmed $20.02 material cost. All 6 confirmed $0 file prep.

**Wave 2 — Destruction (6 models, hostile attack vectors):**
- Buyer/Procurement: 6/6 HIGH — flat $/sq ft across size classes is the structural error.
- Competitor: 6/6 HIGH — regional competitor at $55–$68 qty 20, $44–$50 qty 200+.
- Cost Auditor: 4/6 MEDIUM, 2/6 HIGH — 6" spacing padding ($0.40–$0.65), margin is material-only.
- Strategic: 6/6 HIGH — must establish separate large-format size class before first invoice.
- Verdicts: 6/6 rejected original table ($91 at qty 20).

**Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models as Sean Finn):**
- All 6 pushed back. None approved as-is at $85.
- Pushback threshold range: $75–$85. Central cluster: $80–$82.
- Instant approval range: $65–$78. Central cluster: $78 (4 of 6).
- Incumbent estimate: $55–$70. All 6 referenced Pro Sign.
- PO decision: All 6 held PO. All 6 would send question/counteroffer email.
- All 6 confirmed this becomes a permanent data point in the buyer's pricing model.

**Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, binary verdicts):**
- Verdict: **6/6 NO on $85 at qty 20.**
- All 6 recommended **$78 at qty 20. UNANIMOUS.**
- All 6 required separate large-format size class established before first invoice.

**Nick's Final Decision:**
- Accepted $78 at qty 20 (unanimous Wave 4 recommendation).
- Set T1 at $105, T2 at $92 (operational tiers — Sean orders across T1–T3).
- Set T4–T6 at $68/$60/$52 for structural completeness.
- No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process.

**AI Validation Summary:** 24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves. Unanimous on $78 at qty 20 in Wave 4. Unanimous on the requirement to establish a separate large-format size class before first invoice.

**Override:** None. Engine consensus accepted.

**Profile Implication:** This item establishes the founding data point for the Large-Format Cut Vinyl band (5+ sq ft) at $11.03/sq ft at qty 20. Future cut vinyl items at ≥ 5.0 sq ft validate against this band, NOT the 2.51–2.56 sq ft concession band.

- **Validation height vs operative height.** Validation was conducted using an initial height of 13.5" (6.639 sq ft). Nick subsequently corrected the operative height to 14.375" (7.069 sq ft). All prices and margins are unchanged because material cost is feed-length-driven, not area-driven. The vinyl cost ($18.51) depends on the 76.8125" feed length, which is unchanged. The tape cost ($1.51) depends on the same feed length and still nests 2-up on the 30" tape (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" ≤ 30"). The only figures that changed are sq ft (6.639 → 7.069), $/sq ft at each tier (recalculated with larger denominator), and the benchmark step-down percentage (14% → 19.3%). The $78 at qty 20 was validated unanimously by all 6 Wave 4 models.

sections.nesting:
## Roll Specs

| Spec | Value |
|------|-------|
| Roll | 3M Controltac 180mC-57 Olympic Blue, 24" × 10yd |
| Roll cost | $162.78 |
| Roll sq in | 24" × 360" = 8,640 sq in |
| Cost per sq in | $162.78 ÷ 8,640 = $0.018839/sq in |
| Cost per sq ft | $162.78 ÷ 60 sq ft = $2.713/sq ft |
| Cost per yard | $162.78 ÷ 10 yd = $16.278/yd |

## Nesting Layout on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label width (across roll) | 11.00" |
| Labels across 24" roll | 2 (2 × 11" = 22", leaves 2" waste strip) |
| Label length (down roll) | 32.88" = 0.9133 yd |
| Positions along 360" roll | 10 (360 ÷ 32.88 = 10.95 → 10 complete) |
| **Labels per roll** | **20 (2 across × 10 positions)** |
| Unused at roll end | 360" − (10 × 32.88") = 31.2" |
| Roll-end waste | 8.7% of roll length |
| Side waste | 2" strip (9.1% of roll width) |

> **Yield Note:** The session brief indicated "10 labels per roll (2 across × 5 positions)." Verified math: 360" ÷ 32.88" = 10.95 → 10 full positions. Correct yield is **20 labels per roll**. Material cost per label (calculated from $/sq in × allocated area) is unaffected by this discrepancy.

## Material Cost per Label

**Vinyl (length-based, includes blade path allocation):**

| Step | Value |
|------|-------|
| Roll width allocated per label | 24" ÷ 2 = 12" (2-across layout) |
| Label length | 32.88" = 0.9133 yd |
| Cost per row of 2 labels | 0.9133 yd × $16.278/yd = $14.864 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $14.864 ÷ 2 = **$7.43** |

**Application Tape:**

| Step | Value |
|------|-------|
| Label area | 32.88" × 11.00" / 144 = 2.512 sq ft |
| Tape cost | 2.512 sq ft × $0.5911/sq ft = **$1.485** |

**Total Material Cost per Label: $7.43 + $1.485 = $8.915 → $8.92**

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-57 Olympic Blue) | $7.43 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U) | $1.485 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$8.915 → $8.92** |

> **No 48" roll option available for this color.** Olympic Blue is only stocked at 24" × 10yd. If higher volume warrants it, a 48" roll quote from 3M would reduce material cost — document as an operational improvement opportunity, not current pricing basis.

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Olympic Blue stock. 2 labels across, cut in rows. Estimated: 5-8 min per production run.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around "D115" block letters. 4 large characters, no fine detail, open geometry. Estimated: 10-15 min per unit at small quantities; 5-8 min/unit at production volume.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. Squeegee for adhesion. Estimated: 3-5 min per label.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, confirm no lifted edges, check character legibility.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier. Label is delivered ready for hand-application on equipment.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" stock)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low. "D115" is 4 large block characters at generous scale. No serifs, no fine interior detail. Similar weeding profile to "E190" (P/N 1205720).
- **Weeding time:** Faster than the 7-character "ELLIOTT" brand lettering (P/N 3017435). Comparable to P/N 1205720.

## Estimated Production Time per Unit

| Step | Time (small qty) | Time (production vol) |
|------|-----------------|----------------------|
| File import/setup | ~5 min mechanical, NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) | — |
| Cut | 8 min | 5 min |
| Weed | 15 min | 8 min |
| Mask (transfer tape) | 5 min | 3 min |
| Inspect + pack | 5 min | 3 min |
| **Per-unit billable total** | **~33 min** | **~19 min** |

Per PRICING_VALIDATION.md, multi-round validation is exempt for items dimensionally and materially identical to an already-quoted item. Profile band validation still applies and was performed.

**Override:** None. Engine output.

Rule 14 deviation acknowledged: P/N 1205720 is a Relationship Concession. This item prices within the concession-phase band by deliberate strategic choice, not oversight. If/when relationship pricing normalizes, recalibrate against the AI consensus range ($14.84–$16.41/sq ft).
